column2: A company must remove all existing data from a working drive and then reuse the drive. Should it choose logical wiping or physical destruction?
column5: Physical destruction makes the drive unusable. A whole-drive wipe addresses the stored data while preserving a working device for recycling or repurposing.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield DataSanitization Repurposing Comparison Source::Messer-v140

column2: When a hard drive is destroyed by drilling, what must the drill pass through to damage the data-bearing media?
column4: The drive platters, not merely the outer case.
column5: The purpose of drilling is physical media damage. A hole that affects only the enclosure does not accomplish that goal.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic PhysicalDestruction Drilling HardDrives Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization is retiring many drives and wants heavy machinery to break the media into pieces so the drives cannot be reused. Which destruction method matches this requirement?
column5: Shredding is a physical-destruction method. It fits a permanent retirement decision, unlike wiping or a regular format that preserves a usable drive.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield PhysicalDestruction Shredding Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A magnetic hard drive must be sanitized and permanently rendered unusable by removing its magnetic field. Which method should be used?
column5: Degaussing acts on magnetic storage. It is destructive to a hard drive rather than a reuse-oriented erasing method.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield Degaussing MagneticMedia Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why is degaussing not an effective destruction method for an SSD or other flash storage?
column4: Flash storage does not store data with the magnetic field that degaussing removes.
column5: Match the destruction method to the media technology. If flash media must be physically destroyed, do not choose degaussing; use an approved method compatible with nonmagnetic storage.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield Degaussing SSD FlashStorage Comparison Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which listed physical-destruction method uses intense heat to destroy storage media?
column5: Incineration belongs with drilling, shredding, and degaussing in the physical-destruction category; it does not preserve a reusable drive.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic PhysicalDestruction Incineration Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician overwrites one sensitive file on a drive. What important limitation distinguishes this from a whole-drive wipe?
column4: The other files on the drive remain available.
column5: File-level overwriting has a narrow scope. It does not satisfy a requirement to sanitize every previously stored item before the drive leaves the organization.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic DataErasure FileOverwrite Comparison Source::Messer-v140

column2: A working drive will be assigned to a new purpose, and all of its current data must be removed. Which erasure scope matches this requirement?
column5: A file-level overwrite affects only selected files. Whole-drive wiping matches the full-media scope while leaving the drive available for reuse.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield DataWiping Repurposing Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why is low-level formatting not a normal end-user data-destruction choice?
column4: It is a factory-provided process rather than a recommended user operation.
column5: Do not choose low-level formatting merely because its name sounds more thorough. For reuse, compare approved wiping and regular formatting based on their actual overwrite behavior.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic Formatting LowLevelFormat Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why should a quick standard format not be treated as proof that a drive's old data was sanitized?
column4: It clears file-system indexing information without overwriting the existing data, so recovery may still be possible.
column5: The word format does not by itself establish sanitization. The overwrite behavior distinguishes quick formatting from the regular option described by the source.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield Formatting QuickFormat DataRecovery Comparison Source::Messer-v140

column2: At the source's stated Windows scope, which standard-format option overwrites the drive's sectors with zeros while keeping the drive usable?
column5: A quick format clears the file table but does not overwrite the old data. The regular option is the reuse-oriented choice when sector overwriting is required.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield Formatting RegularFormat Repurposing Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A third-party vendor destroys or recycles an organization's drives. What document should the organization require as evidence of the completed work?
column5: Outsourcing can provide equipment and scale, but the organization still needs a record of the result. The certificate creates the paper trail for what happened to the media.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield ThirdParty CertificateOfDestruction Documentation Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Before selecting or outsourcing a storage-disposal method, what external requirements must the organization identify?
column5: Local rules and policies may legally require storage drives to be destroyed. The applicable regulatory and environmental requirements must be checked rather than assuming one universal process.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 45
column10: A+::220-1202::2.9 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::DataDestructionandDisposal Basic HighYield Compliance Regulations Environmental Disposal Source::Messer-v140
